---
title: "Quarto 3 — Figures"
subtitle: "A readable plot, caption, alt text, and label"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## The required outcome — 45 minutes

> Add one `ggplot2` figure with clear axes, a caption, `fig-alt`, and a `fig-` label.

Advanced composition, interactive plots, and manuscript export are demonstrations.

# Figure options {.section-divider}

## Creation and display size

| Option | Controls |
|---|---|
| `fig-width` | Width at creation, in inches |
| `fig-height` | Height at creation, in inches |
| `out-width` | Display width in the output |
| `fig-align` | Display alignment |

## A cross-reference-ready figure

```` markdown
```{r}`r ''`
#| label: fig-pfoa-cholesterol
#| fig-cap: "Simulated serum PFOA and total cholesterol."
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of simulated serum PFOA against total cholesterol, with points distinguished by site."
#| echo: false

# ggplot code
```
````

::: {.fragment}
Do not type “Figure 1” in the caption. Quarto numbers it.
:::

# Accessible visual encoding {.section-divider}

## Do not rely on color alone

```r
ggplot(dat, aes(
  pfoa_serum,
  cholesterol,
  color = site,
  shape = site
)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65)
```

::: {.incremental}
- Use informative axis labels and units.
- Combine color with shape, line type, labels, or facets.
- Write alt text that communicates the figure's purpose and visible pattern.
:::

# Advanced demonstrations {.section-divider}

## Know the tradeoffs

::: {.incremental}
- `patchwork` combines static ggplots.
- Facets repeat the same visual grammar across groups.
- `plotly` adds HTML interaction and needs a static fallback for other formats.
- `ggsave()` creates a separate submission file when a journal requires one.
:::

# Scientific boundary {.section-divider}

## The pattern is generated

The workshop data are synthetic. A visible fitted trend is an instructional feature, not scientific evidence.

::: {.callout-important}
Describe what the teaching plot displays without making a causal or substantive PFAS claim.
:::

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Add the required figure

In `student_work/my_report.qmd`, plot `pfoa_serum` against `cholesterol` and add:

1. Clear labels.
2. `fig-pfoa-cholesterol`.
3. An informative caption.
4. Useful alt text.
5. Color plus shape when mapping site.

Recovery: `student_work/checkpoints/03_figure_complete.qmd`.
